data.title: Adidas Targets High Single-Digit Growth in 2025, Building on Strong 2024 Performance
data.description:
Adidas, the global sportswear giant, is poised for continued growth in 2025, with the company targeting a high single-digit increase in currency-neutral revenues. This optimistic outlook follows a strong 2024 performance, marked by double-digit revenue growth and significant improvements in gross margin and operating profit. Adidas' CEO, Bjørn Gulden, expressed confidence in the brand's momentum and potential for further market share gains in key regions.



Adidas' 2024 results were driven by a broad-based increase in footwear sales, with double-digit growth in Originals, Football, Training, Sportswear, Running, and Performance Basketball. The company's ability to innovate and meet consumer needs across various price points contributed to this success. In 2025, Adidas expects to continue this trend with a high-single-digit increase in currency-neutral revenues, reflecting continued double-digit growth for the adidas brand (Gulden, 2025).

Adidas' gross margin improved by 3.3 percentage points to 50.8% in 2024, despite significant negative currency effects. This improvement was driven by lower product and freight costs, a more favorable business mix, and reduced discounting. In Q4 2024, the gross margin improved by 5.2 percentage points to 49.8%. Adidas expects to maintain this trend in 2025, with an operating profit increase to a level of between €1.7 billion and €1.8 billion (Gulden, 2025).

Adidas' strong performance in 2024 was also driven by an increase in market share across all markets. The company grew double digits in Europe, North America, Greater China, Emerging Markets, and Latin America. This growth was achieved by going into the market with products relevant to local consumers, demonstrating the company's agile and local approach. Adidas expects to continue increasing market share in 2025, with a goal to grow at least 10% with the adidas brand (Gulden, 2025).



Adidas' strategic focus on sustainability and local consumer relevance has significantly contributed to its growth potential in key markets like North America, Europe, and Greater China. By addressing the growing demand for sustainable products and catering to the diverse needs of local consumers, Adidas has been able to maintain a strong competitive position and achieve significant growth in these markets.

In conclusion, Adidas' strong performance in 2024 and its optimistic outlook for 2025 reflect the company's ability to innovate, adapt to market demands, and execute strategic initiatives. With a focus on sustainability, local consumer relevance, and strategic partnerships, Adidas is well-positioned to continue driving brand heat and market share in key regions.

data.title: Meta's Facial Recognition Test Expands to the UK: A Step Towards Enhanced Fraud Prevention
data.description:
Meta, the parent company of facebook and Instagram, has taken a significant step in its global anti-fraud strategy by expanding its facial recognition test to the United Kingdom. This move comes after the company engaged with regulators in the UK and received the green light to proceed with the test. The facial recognition technology aims to combat the growing issue of "celeb bait" scams, which exploit the likenesses of famous people without their consent to promote fraudulent investment schemes and cryptocurrencies.

The expansion to the UK is a strategic move for meta, as it seeks to address the concerns of public figures who have been victimized by these scams. By offering them the option to opt-in to the "celeb-bait protection" with facial recognition technology, Meta can help safeguard their images from being misused in scam ads. This approach demonstrates the company's commitment to engaging with regulators and addressing societal concerns about data privacy.

However, the expansion to the UK also presents potential risks for Meta. The company has faced regulatory challenges and lawsuits related to its facial recognition technology in the past. In 2021, Meta shut down its decade-old facial recognition tool for photos due to privacy and regulatory backlash. Additionally, the company agreed to pay $1.4 billion to settle a lawsuit in Texas over inappropriate biometric data collection related to its facial recognition technology. To mitigate these risks, Meta has emphasized that the facial recognition tools will be optional and that it will delete any facial data generated from ads after a one-time comparison.



The expansion of Meta's facial recognition test to the UK is a strategic move in the company's global anti-fraud strategy, as it aims to protect public figures and users from "celeb bait" scams. While there are potential benefits, such as addressing the concerns of public figures and engaging with regulators, there are also risks associated with the use of facial recognition technology. To minimize these risks, Meta has emphasized the optional nature of the tools and the deletion of facial data after a one-time comparison.

As Meta continues to expand its facial recognition test to more countries, it will likely face similar regulatory challenges and considerations. In Europe, for instance, Meta has yet to launch the facial recognition tool, and it is unclear whether the company has received regulatory approval or is currently seeking it. The European Union has strict data protection laws, such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), which could pose additional challenges for Meta's facial recognition expansion. The company may need to ensure that its facial recognition tools comply with these regulations and address any concerns related to data privacy, accuracy, and potential biases in the technology.

In conclusion, the expansion of Meta's facial recognition test to the UK is a significant step in the company's global anti-fraud strategy. By addressing the concerns of public figures and engaging with regulators, Meta demonstrates its commitment to combating fraudulent activities on its platforms. However, the company must also address the potential risks associated with facial recognition technology and ensure that its tools comply with local laws and regulations as it expands to other regions.

data.title: Crypto Godfather's Ex-Girlfriend Admits to Tax Evasion
data.description:
The former girlfriend of Adam Iza, a notorious figure in the cryptocurrency world known as the "crypto godfather," has pleaded guilty to a federal criminal tax charge. Iris Ramaya Au, 35, admitted to failing to report over $2.6 million in "ill-gotten gains" derived from her ex-boyfriend's criminal operations.

Au now faces up to three years in federal prison for her role in facilitating Iza's fraudulent activities. From 2020 to 2023, Au used shell companies and illicitly obtained funds to finance a lavish lifestyle alongside Iza, including luxury cars, real estate, and cryptocurrency.

Au's agreement with the Department of Justice revealed she transferred illicit funds into her personal accounts, failing to report this income to the Internal Revenue Service. Prosecutors allege she was aware of the origin of the funds.

In September 2024, Iza, 24, was arrested and charged with conspiracy, wire fraud, and tax evasion, all linked to a series of criminal activities stretching over several years. Over the course of their scheme, the couple spent approximately $10 million on luxury recreational activities, and Iza acquired $16 million in crypto with the fraudulent funds, prosecutors said.

Iza's criminal career peaked when he used his platform, Zort, a crypto trading platform, to carry out large-scale fraud, including defrauding meta of $37 million by manipulating facebook advertising accounts. Authorities allege Iza also stole significant amounts of crypto by engaging in violent activities, including a failed home invasion in 2022.

In 2018, he allegedly impersonated the FBI to steal crypto at gunpoint, holding a victim and his girlfriend hostage while demanding laptop passwords. In January, the crypto criminal pled guilty to conspiracy, wire fraud, and tax evasion. In his plea, Iza admitted to using off-duty Los Angeles County Sheriff's deputies to harass his business rivals and engage in unlawful activities, including fabricating search warrants.

One of the deputies, Eric Chase Saavedra, is also facing charges, over allegations he misused his position to assist Iza in intimidating victims and stealing confidential law enforcement information.

data.title: Russia's Polyus Reports Record EBITDA of $5.7 Billion in 2024
data.description:
Russia's Polyus, the country's largest gold producer, has reported a record EBITDA of $5.7 billion in 2024, marking a significant increase from the previous year. The company's strong performance was driven by higher gold sales volumes and more favorable gold prices. Polyus' EBITDA margin in 2024 was 70.30%, which is significantly higher than the industry average of around 40-50% for gold mining companies. This indicates a strong operational performance compared to its peers.

Polyus' exceptional EBITDA growth in 2024 was driven by several key factors:

1. Increased gold sales volumes: Polyus sold 1,263 thousand ounces of gold in the first half of 2024, which was broadly flat compared to the same period in 2023. However, the company accumulated inventories of around 210 thousand ounces of gold content, mainly in the form of floatation concentrate, which will be sold throughout the second half of the year. This will contribute to higher gold sales volumes and, consequently, higher EBITDA in the full year of 2024.
2. Higher average realized refined gold price: The average realized refined gold price in the first half of 2024 was higher compared to the same period in 2023. This increase in the average gold price positively impacted Polyus' revenue and, ultimately, its EBITDA.
3. Rouble depreciation: The depreciation of the Russian rouble during the reporting period had a positive effect on Polyus' EBITDA. A weaker rouble makes exports more competitive, which can lead to higher sales and profits for the company.
4. Higher head grades at certain mines: Polyus noted higher head grades at Blagodatnoye, Natalka, and Kuranakh, which contributed to lower cash costs and higher EBITDA.

However, the sustainability of these factors in the long term is uncertain:

* Gold sales volumes: While Polyus has accumulated inventories of gold content, these inventories are not infinite. Once they are sold, the company will need to maintain or increase its production levels to sustain higher gold sales volumes.
* Gold price: The gold price is subject to market fluctuations and is influenced by various global factors. While Polyus may benefit from higher gold prices in the short term, there is no guarantee that this trend will continue in the long term.
* Rouble depreciation: The rouble's value is influenced by various economic and political factors. While a weaker rouble can be beneficial for Polyus, a significant and sustained depreciation could also lead to higher input costs, potentially offsetting the benefits.
* Head grades: Head grades can vary from year to year and are influenced by the mining sequence and the depletion of high-grade ore bodies. Polyus has already indicated that it expects a planned decline in grades in ore mined at Olimpiada, which could negatively impact its cash costs and EBITDA in the future.

In conclusion, while the factors contributing to Polyus' exceptional EBITDA growth in 2024 are significant, their long-term sustainability is uncertain and will depend on various internal and external factors. Investors should closely monitor the company's performance and the broader gold market to assess the potential for continued growth and value creation.

data.title: Jim Cramer on why investors need to stay on their toes with tariff talk
US Jobless Claims Rise to 242,000, Raising Investor Concerns Over Labor Market Weakness
data.description:
Converted Markdown

The Bottom Line Up Front:New unemployment filings surged to 242,000 this week – the highest level since December 2024 and well above Wall Street’s 221,000 forecast. This surprise jump signals growing stress in the job market, forcing investors to rethink bets on Federal Reserve rate cuts and resilient corporate earnings. Here’s what you need to know.

Why This Matters Right Now

The labor market has been the bedrock of the U.S. economy’s strength, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits even as inflation stayed stubborn. But cracks are showing:

  1. Fed Policy Shift? The Fed has held rates steady since July 2024, but rising job losses could push them to cut rates sooner to prevent a deeper downturn.
  2. Recession Watch: The Sahm Rule – which flags recessions when unemployment spikes – is flashing yellow. Today’s data brings us closer to that trigger.
  3. Portfolio Impact: Stocks slid on the news, while bonds rallied as traders sought safety. Sector winners/losers are shifting fast.

Breaking Down the Jobless Claims Data

What Are Jobless Claims?

Jobless claims track how many Americans file for unemployment benefits each week. It’s split into two numbers:

  • Initial claims: First-time filers (this week: 242,000)
  • Continuing claims: People still receiving benefits (this week: 1.87 million)

Think of it as a real-time pulse check on layoffs. When claims rise, it means more people are losing jobs – and that often hints at bigger economic trouble ahead14.

This Week’s Surprise Spike

MetricThis WeekLast WeekEconomist ForecastInitial Claims242,000220,000221,0004-Week Average224,000215,500–

Key Takeaway: Claims haven’t been this high since the holiday season. The 4-week average (which smooths weekly swings) also hit a 3-month high45.

What’s Driving the Layoffs?

  • Corporate Cutbacks: Tech, retail, and manufacturing companies are trimming staff to protect profits as sales slow.
  • Government Layoffs: Thousands of federal workers lost jobs under a new “efficiency program,” but these numbers aren’t fully reflected in today’s report5.
  • Weather Woes: Severe winter storms forced temporary shutdowns in multiple states, pushing claims higher4.

Investor Implications: 3 Ways This Shakes Markets

1. Fed Rate Cut Odds Rise (But It’s Complicated)

The Fed wants to see inflation fall and employment stay strong. With prices still rising faster than their 2% target (CPI was 3.1% in January), today’s weak jobs data puts them in a tough spot:

  • Dovish Scenario: If unemployment keeps rising, expect rate cuts by June to stimulate hiring.
  • Hawkish Reality: Services inflation (think healthcare, rents) remains hot, which could delay cuts despite job market pain24.

Market Move: After the report, traders priced in a 40% chance of a June rate cut – up from 28% last week.

2. Sector Rotations Accelerate

  • Losers: Cyclical stocks tied to economic growth – like industrials (‑0.8%) and consumer discretionary (‑1.1%) – sold off.
  • Winners: “Safe haven” assets like utilities (+1.2%) and gold (+0.9%) gained as investors hedged recession risks45.

3. Bond Market Sees Mixed Signals

  • Shorter-Term Bonds: 2-year Treasury yields fell to 4.35% as rate-cut hopes grew.
  • Longer-Term Bonds: 10-year yields dropped to 4.05%, reflecting worries about slower growth ahead45.

The Big Debate: Blip or Breakdown?

Bull Case: Temporary Glitch

  • Weather and seasonal adjustments distorted the numbers.
  • Continuing claims (people staying unemployed) barely moved.
  • Job openings still outnumber unemployed workers 1.4-to-1.

Bear Case: Trend Shift

  • Layoffs are spreading beyond tech to “Main Street” sectors.
  • The 4-week claims average has risen 11% since January.
  • Consumer confidence just hit a 9-month low – a red flag for spending.

Your Action Plan

  1. Revisit Defense: Boost exposure to sectors like healthcare, utilities, and consumer staples that weather downturns better.
  2. Watch the Fed: The March 20 FOMC meeting could hint at policy shifts – listen for any mention of labor market risks.
  3. Next Data Drop: The March 8 jobs report will show if unemployment rose past 4%. A move to 4.2% would trigger the Sahm Rule’s recession warning45.

Bottom Line for InvestorsToday’s jobless claims report doesn’t guarantee a recession, but it’s a warning sign that the economy’s “immune system” is weakening. With stocks near record highs and bond markets pricing in rate cuts, positioning your portfolio for volatility is key. Stick with quality companies (strong balance sheets, steady cash flows) and keep some dry powder to buy dips if the jobs slump deepens.

⁂

data.title: What to Expect from Trump's Congress Speech?
data.description:
President Donald Trump is set to deliver a powerful speech before Congress on Tuesday at 9 PM Eastern Time, centered around the theme of MAGA. This address will provide insight into the agenda of the new administration. 

Here's what to expect from Trump's speech:

Addressing High Costs and Tariffs

The U.S. continues to struggle with high inflation. Since taking office, Trump has blamed high prices on his predecessor, Joe Biden, but has yet to propose concrete measures to lower costs. Some Republicans worry that inflation could pose a political risk, and Trump's tariff hikes may further drive up prices.

In his speech, Trump is expected to outline his administration's approach to tackling inflation and justify the necessity of tariffs.

Immigration Policy: Historic Mass Deportation Plan

Trump is advancing the largest illegal immigrant deportation effort in U.S. history. The Department of Homeland Security and the Department of Defense have requested additional funding to deport millions of undocumented immigrants. However, Trump and his top officials remain dissatisfied with the current pace of arrests by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).

Trump is likely to reiterate his commitment to strengthening border security and push Congress for additional funding to build more border barriers. Meanwhile, Democratic-led states like California and New York have vowed to challenge his policies and protect undocumented immigrants.

Ukraine and Middle East Policy

Last Friday, Trump had a heated exchange with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House over the ongoing Ukraine war, drawing global attention.

Trump's shifting stance on Ukraine is seen as a pivotal moment for U.S.-Europe relations. The future of U.S. military aid to Ukraine remains uncertain, and European nations fear Trump may reduce or even cut off support.

In his speech, Trump is expected to discuss his approach to facilitating a ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, as well as possible reductions in funding and military assistance. He may also emphasize Europe's need to take on greater responsibility, pressuring the UK, France, and Germany to increase their aid to Ukraine. Additionally, U.S.-Ukraine mineral agreements may be mentioned.

Trump's remarks could set a new direction for U.S. policy on Ukraine and have significant geopolitical implications.

Meanwhile, he is also expected to address his strategy for ending the ongoing conflict between Israel and Hamas. Since returning to the White House, Trump has met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, the first foreign leader to visit him in Washington. Trump has repeatedly stated that the U.S. will exert control over Gaza.

Government Restructuring: Mass Federal Layoffs

The Trump administration is undergoing the largest federal restructuring in U.S. history, including the dismissal of thousands of federal employees and a reduction in government agencies.

The restructuring is being spearheaded by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk, aiming to streamline operations and cut redundant expenses. However, DOGE's aggressive approach has raised concerns among voters, with many writing to their congressional representatives questioning Musk's decisions. Additionally, outsourcing government functions to private firms has sparked debates over data security risks.

Musk is expected to attend the speech as a guest in the congressional gallery. Trump may use his speech to defend the department's reforms and reassure moderate Republicans and independents, though strong criticism from Democrats is anticipated.

data.title: Tesla's $1,100 Insurance Subsidy: A Game Changer for Model 3 in China?
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) has rolled out an enticing offer for Chinese customers, providing a $1,100 insurance subsidy for the purchase of any Model 3 variant. This limited-time promotion, available until February 28, 2025, aims to boost demand and alleviate delivery hardships in the first quarter. But how effective will this strategy be in the face of dwindling demand, price increases, and intense competition from local EV manufacturers?



Tesla's insurance subsidy, worth RMB 8,000 ($1,100), is available for customers who order any variant of the Model 3 before February 28, 2025. This promotion is designed to make the Model 3 more affordable and stimulate demand during the slow season for car sales in China. While this move is commendable, it may not be enough to significantly impact Tesla's delivery challenges in the first quarter.

Tesla's delivery challenges in China can be attributed to several factors, including dwindling demand, price increases, and intense competition from local EV manufacturers. Although the insurance subsidy is a step in the right direction, it may not be sufficient to address these underlying issues. Moreover, the subsidy is only applicable to the Model 3, which might not create a significant halo effect for other tesla models.

Tesla's market share in China has been steadily growing, reaching 8.8% in 2024. However, the global annual deliveries fell compared to 2023, indicating a potential slowdown in demand. The insurance subsidy could help Tesla regain some of that lost momentum and increase its market share in China. Nevertheless, the impact of this promotion is likely to be limited, as suggested by Tesla researcher Troy Teslike.

In conclusion, Tesla's insurance subsidy for the Model 3 in China is a strategic move to boost demand and alleviate delivery hardships in the first quarter. While this promotion is expected to have a limited impact, it demonstrates Tesla's commitment to maintaining its competitive edge in the Chinese EV market. As the competition intensifies, Tesla must continue to innovate and adapt to remain at the forefront of the industry.

data.title: Whale's $19.3M SOL Sell Orders Spark Market Concern
data.description:
A significant development in the cryptocurrency market has caught the attention of investors and enthusiasts alike. A prominent whale, a term used to describe large-scale investors in the crypto world, has recently undelegated $19.3 million worth of SOL (Solana) and placed multiple limit sell orders above the current price. This move has sparked speculation and discussion within the community, with some expressing concerns about the potential impact on the SOL price.

In response to the growing interest in his actions, cz, the CEO of Binance, has been actively engaging with the community on Twitter. He has been addressing various questions and concerns, demonstrating his commitment to maintaining open communication with the crypto community. This approach has been well-received, with many appreciating his efforts to keep the dialogue going.

The news of the whale's actions comes amidst a backdrop of rumors and speculation about potential changes in cryptocurrency regulations. Some have been discussing the possibility of Trump's cancellation of the cryptocurrency capital gains tax becoming a reality. While these rumors are still unconfirmed, they highlight the ongoing uncertainty and volatility in the crypto market.

Meanwhile, in a separate development, a new cryptocurrency called "Wen's Coin" has been making waves in the market. In a feud between the US and Ukrainian presidents, this coin has quickly gained traction, reaching a market cap of $35 million. This rapid growth has caught the attention of investors and has sparked discussions about the potential of new cryptocurrencies in the market.

In another notable development, the DeFi (decentralized finance) sector has been exploring new ways to distribute value to its users. The aave buyback proposal, which aims to introduce dividends to the DeFi ecosystem, has been a topic of discussion among investors and developers. This move could potentially open up new avenues for value creation and distribution in the DeFi space.

data.title: "Brother of Breakdancer Charged in Crypto Scam: ASIC Takes Action"
data.description:
Australian authorities have charged Brendan Gunn, the brother of renowned breakdancer Rachael "Raygun" Gunn, with allegedly dealing with proceeds of crime linked to cryptocurrency. The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) accused Gunn of handling two bank cheques totaling $181,000, which were deposited by three investors for conversion into cryptocurrency.

Gunn, a director at Mormarkets, a company that accepted deposits for conversion to crypto, allegedly attempted to open multiple bank accounts for the firm despite repeated closures due to scam concerns. He appeared in court on March 4 and is expected to return on April 29. If convicted, Gunn faces up to three years in prison and a fine of $37,800.

ASIC Chair Joe Longo emphasized the regulator's commitment to preventing and detecting scams, stating that they will take action when necessary. This comes as Australian regulators have been shifting their focus towards the crypto industry, with the Australian Transaction Reports and Analysis Center (AUSTRAC) taking action against several remittance service providers and crypto exchanges in recent months.

In December 2024, AUSTRAC CEO Brendan Thomas announced the Anti-Money Laundering regulator's intention to prioritize the cryptocurrency industry in 2025. ASIC also released a consultation paper on proposed guidance for crypto, categorizing many digital assets as financial products and requiring firms dealing in crypto to obtain a license.

data.title: Progressive's Q4 2024 Earnings Call: A Deep Dive into Strategic Initiatives
data.description:
As the dust settles on Progressive's Q4 2024 earnings call, investors are eager to understand the strategic initiatives that have driven the company's success. In this article, we will delve into the key takeaways from the call and explore how Progressive's focus on people and culture, competitive pricing, and technology investments in the claims process have contributed to its financial performance.



Progressive's CEO, Tricia Griffith, kicked off the call by highlighting two strategic pillars that have been instrumental in the company's growth: people and culture, and competitive pricing with a focus on technology investments in the claims process. These pillars have enabled Progressive to maintain a competitive edge in the market and fuel business growth.

People and Culture

Progressive's long history of success begins with its people and culture. The company has consistently invested in its employees, fostering a culture of innovation and continuous improvement. This was evident in the call, as John Murphy, Progressive's claims president, emphasized the importance of people and culture in driving the company's success.

One example of Progressive's commitment to its employees is the appointment of Matt White as the claims business leader for data and analytics. Matt joined Progressive in 2014 as an IT manager and has since risen through the ranks, demonstrating the company's dedication to developing its talent. Matt's leadership in data and analytics has been crucial in enhancing Progressive's claims team's accuracy and efficiency.

Competitive Pricing

Progressive has maintained competitive pricing, which has helped attract and retain customers. The company's pricing strategy, combined with its strong claims handling capabilities, has contributed to its financial performance in 2024. Progressive's ability to offer competitive prices while maintaining profitability can be attributed to its efficient claims process and effective use of technology.

Technology Investments in Claims Process

Progressive has invested heavily in technology to enhance its claims process, making it more accurate and efficient. This investment has helped the company maintain a competitive advantage in the market and contributed to its financial performance. One example of this investment is the use of predictive analytics to identify potential fraudulent claims.

Matt White mentioned that Progressive uses machine learning algorithms to analyze patterns and detect anomalies in claims data, enabling them to flag suspicious claims for further investigation. This proactive approach helps Progressive to reduce the number of fraudulent claims, ultimately saving the company money and improving the overall claims process efficiency.



In conclusion, Progressive's strategic initiatives in 2024, focusing on people and culture, competitive pricing, and technology investments in the claims process, have had a positive impact on the company's financial performance. These initiatives have helped Progressive maintain a competitive edge in the market and contributed to its success in 2024. As the company continues to invest in its employees, pricing strategy, and technology, investors can expect Progressive to remain a strong player in the insurance industry.

data.title: Y-mAbs Therapeutics: A Promising Biopharma Play in 2025
data.description:
Y-mAbs Therapeutics, Inc. (YMAB), a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on radioimmunotherapy and antibody-based therapeutic cancer products, recently reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2024. The company's strategic realignment, announced in January 2025, has positioned it to drive future growth with its flagship product, DANYELZA, while accelerating the preclinical and clinical advancement of its Self-Assembly DisAssembly Pre-targeted Technology platform (SADA PRIT) and other pipeline assets. Here's a closer look at Y-mAbs Therapeutics' recent performance and growth prospects.



Financial Performance and Growth Prospects

Y-mAbs Therapeutics reported total revenues of $26.5 million for the fourth quarter of 2024 and $87.7 million for the full year 2024. The company's cash and cash equivalents held as of December 31, 2024, reflect a total annual cash investment of $11.4 million in the full year 2024. Management has announced full-year 2025 guidance around total revenue, operating expenses, and cash investment, as well as first-quarter 2025 guidance around total revenue.

The company's strategic realignment, which involved the establishment of two business units—DANYELZA and Radiopharmaceuticals—has enhanced its ability to execute on its business goals. This move has allowed y-mabs therapeutics to optimize its internal resources and provide flexibility and agility to advance its novel SADA PRIT programs through clinical development while simultaneously driving commercial growth of DANYELZA.



Key Drivers and Challenges

1. Ex-U.S. Growth and U.S. Market Competition: Y-mAbs Therapeutics experienced solid ex-U.S. growth in 2024, driven by exclusive license and distribution agreements and strong partner performance. To maintain this momentum in the face of increased competition in the U.S. market, the company can focus on strengthening its commercial operations, diversifying its product offerings, optimizing pricing and reimbursement strategies, and generating robust clinical data to support DANYELZA's efficacy and safety.
2. Radiopharmaceuticals Business Unit: The establishment of the Radiopharmaceuticals business unit has contributed to the advancement of the company's SADA PRIT platform. This dedicated focus on preclinical and clinical development has the potential to expand Y-mAbs Therapeutics' product pipeline, enhance its competitive position, and diversify its revenue streams. As the company continues to innovate and bring new products to market, it can strengthen its position in the biopharmaceutical industry and increase its financial stability.
3. Potential Risks and Challenges: While Y-mAbs Therapeutics has made significant strides in its business, there are potential risks and challenges to consider. These include the company's ability to successfully execute on its business goals, maintain its competitive edge in the face of increased competition, and navigate the complexities of clinical development and regulatory approval processes. Additionally, the company's financial performance may be impacted by factors such as changes in the regulatory environment, market dynamics, and the success of its pipeline assets.

In conclusion, Y-mAbs Therapeutics' strategic realignment and strong financial performance position it as a promising biopharma play in 2025. With a focus on driving commercial growth of DANYELZA and accelerating the development of its SADA PRIT platform, the company is well-positioned to capitalize on its strengths and continue its growth trajectory. As investors evaluate the biopharmaceutical landscape, Y-mAbs Therapeutics offers an attractive opportunity for those seeking exposure to a commercial-stage biopharma with a robust pipeline and a commitment to innovation.

data.title: SpaceX's Starship: Launch Delayed, But Ambitions Unchanged
data.description:
Alright, SpaceX fans, buckle up! Elon Musk's rocket manufacturing company has just announced that it's now targeting to launch Starship's eighth flight test as soon as Wednesday. The launch window will open at 5:30 p.m. CT, so mark your calendars!

You might be wondering what happened to the initial launch plan for Monday. Well, SpaceX called off the launch just seconds before liftoff due to an issue with the vehicle. Elon Musk took to social media to explain that there were "too many question marks about this flight" and that they were 20 bar low on ground spin start pressure. So, they decided to stand down from the launch and inspect both stages.

Now, you might be thinking, "What does this delay mean for SpaceX's timeline and budget?" Well, let me tell you, delays can be costly, both in terms of time and money. Launch delays can lead to increased costs for SpaceX, as each launch attempt requires significant resources and preparation. However, SpaceX has a massive budget for Starship development, with NASA providing billions of dollars to support the project. So, while delays can be frustrating, they're not likely to derail the entire mission.

But what about the long-term goals for Starship? You know, the whole crewed missions to the Moon and Mars thing? Well, let me tell you, this delay isn't going to stop SpaceX from pursuing its dreams. In fact, the upcoming flight test is a crucial step towards those goals. SpaceX is eyeing Starship's first payload deployment and experiments aimed at returning the upper stage of the two-stage vehicle to the launch site for catch. They'll also attempt to return and catch the vehicle's Super Heavy booster, failing which it will have a soft splashdown in the Gulf of America. SpaceX will attempt to deploy four Starlink simulators on its next flight, similar in size to the next generation of Starlink satellites.

So, while the delay might be a setback, it's not the end of the road for SpaceX's Starship. The company remains committed to its ambitious goals for Starship, including crewed missions to the Moon and Mars. And who knows? Maybe this delay will even give them the chance to make some last-minute improvements and modifications to the vehicle, increasing the likelihood of a successful launch.

Now, I know what you're thinking: "But what about the potential implications for SpaceX's timeline and budget?" Well, let me tell you, delays can have an impact on both. The initial launch attempt was scrubbed just seconds before liftoff, pushing the launch to Wednesday, March 5, 2025. This delay comes less than two months after the previous Starship launch on January 16, 2025, which was also scrubbed due to technical issues. These delays may impact the timeline for testing and development of the Starship vehicle, as well as the planned missions for the spacecraft. SpaceX has ambitious goals for Starship, including crewed missions to the Moon and Mars. Delays in testing and development could push back these timelines.

As for the budget, launch delays can lead to increased costs for SpaceX, as each launch attempt requires significant resources and preparation. The scrubbed launch attempt on March 3, 2025, may have resulted in additional expenses for SpaceX, including the cost of de-stacking and re-stacking the Starship rocket system, as well as any necessary repairs or modifications. SpaceX has a significant budget for Starship development, with NASA providing billions of dollars to support the project. However, delays and increased costs could potentially impact the overall budget and funding for the program.

But don't worry, SpaceX fans! Despite these challenges, SpaceX remains committed to its ambitious goals for Starship, including crewed missions to the Moon and Mars. The company may need to adjust its timeline for these missions to account for the delays in testing and development, but that doesn't mean they're giving up on their dreams. In fact, this delay might even give them the chance to make some last-minute improvements and modifications to the vehicle, increasing the likelihood of a successful launch.

So, there you have it, folks! SpaceX's Starship launch has been delayed, but the company's ambitions remain unchanged. They're still aiming for the stars, and I, for one, can't wait to see what they do next. Stay tuned for more updates on the upcoming flight test, and keep your eyes on the sky!

data.title: Target Drops Over 4%, Warns Tariffs May Cut Profits
data.description:
Target released its latest earnings report, warning that it expects "meaningful year-over-year profit pressure" in the first quarter that began on February 2. The company attributed this to several factors, including "tariff uncertainty" and a decline in net sales last month.

In early trading, Target's stock fell over 4%, breaking below its November low. The stock has already lost more than 10% YTD. 

With nearly 2,000 stores, the company is highly exposed to tariff impacts, as more than three-quarters of its revenue comes from general merchandise such as apparel, electronics, and home decor—most of which are imported.

Target forecasted that comparable sales growth in 2025 would be "around flat," marking the third consecutive year of stagnant or declining sales. In 2024, comparable sales rose by just 0.1%, barely surpassing Wall Street's expectations.

Over the past year, Target's stock has declined by 20%, while the S&P 500 Consumer Staples Index has gained 17%. The company has struggled as inflation-weary consumers cut back on discretionary spending. Additionally, target faces growing competition from walmart, which has aggressively targeted high-income shoppers who traditionally favored Target.

"During February, we saw record performance around Valentine's Day. However, our top-line performance for the month was soft, as uncharacteristically cold weather across the US affected apparel sales, and declining consumer confidence impacted our discretionary assortment overall," said Jim Lee, Target's chief financial officer.

In 2024, Target reported an operating margin of 5.2%, down from 5.3% in 2023 and falling short of management's 6% target. The company expects only a "modest increase" in its operating margin this year.

"DeFi Whale Boosts Holdings: $7.5M in AAVE, MKR, ENA, OX, MORPHO"
data.description:
izebel.eth, a prominent player in the decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystem, has recently bolstered its holdings of DeFi tokens. According to data from LookIntoChain, izebel.eth has acquired a significant amount of aave, MKR, ENA, OX, and MORPHO tokens.

In a recent transaction, izebel.eth purchased 20,000 AAVE tokens, valued at approximately $4.25 million. This purchase comes amidst growing interest in AAVE, a leading DeFi platform that enables users to lend and borrow cryptocurrencies. The platform has gained traction due to its innovative use of smart contracts and its role in the burgeoning DeFi sector.

In addition to AAVE, izebel.eth also increased its holdings of Maker (MKR) tokens, acquiring 2,000 MKR tokens worth around $2.75 million. MKR is the governance token of the MakerDAO protocol, which manages the DAI stablecoin. The purchase suggests that izebel.eth is bullish on the future of stablecoins and the broader DeFi ecosystem.

izebel.eth also acquired 3,000,000 ENA tokens, valued at approximately $1.1 million. ENA is the native token of the Enjin ecosystem, a platform that enables the creation and management of blockchain-based assets. The purchase indicates that izebel.eth is diversifying its portfolio to include gaming and non-fungible token (NFT) related assets.

The purchase of 80,000,000 OX tokens, valued at around $741,000, further demonstrates izebel.eth's commitment to the DeFi sector. OX is the governance token of the decentralized exchange (DEX) protocol, SushiSwap. The acquisition suggests that izebel.eth is bullish on the future of decentralized exchanges and the broader DeFi ecosystem.

Lastly, izebel.eth acquired 200,000 MORPHO tokens, valued at approximately $354,000. MORPHO is the governance token of the Morpho protocol, a decentralized platform for creating and managing synthetic assets. The purchase indicates that izebel.eth is interested in the potential of synthetic

data.title: Eli Lilly: A Billionaire's Favorite for Long-Term Growth
data.description:
Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) has caught the attention of prominent billionaire investors, such as ken Fisher and Israel Englander, who have identified the pharmaceutical giant as one of the best long-term investment stocks. With a strong business strategy, robust financial performance, and a commitment to innovation, eli lilly has positioned itself as a leader in the healthcare industry.



Eli Lilly's key products, including Mounjaro, Zepbound, and Trulicity, have driven the company's growth and attracted billionaire investors. Mounjaro and Zepbound, both variations of the same molecule called tirzepatide, are expected to generate on the order of $16 billion in revenue this fiscal year. Morgan Stanley predicts that the annual worldwide sales of obesity drugs will likely swell from last year's tally of less than $10 billion to more than $100 billion by 2030, further boosting the growth potential of these drugs. Trulicity, Eli Lilly's blockbuster diabetes treatment, produced more than $7 billion worth of sales last fiscal year, contributing to the company's strong revenue growth.



Eli Lilly's investment in AI and other innovative technologies positions the company for long-term growth and maintains its competitive edge in the pharmaceutical industry. The company's new AI-driven metabolic analysis platform for diabetes care personalizes glucose management, enhancing medication effectiveness and patient outcomes. By embracing AI, Eli Lilly is at the forefront of the digital health revolution, setting the stage for long-term growth and innovation.



Eli Lilly's strong financial performance, attractive valuation, and commitment to innovation have made it an appealing investment for billionaire investors like Ken Fisher and Israel Englander. The company's diversified product portfolio, robust pipeline, and long-term growth potential have positioned Eli Lilly as a leader in the healthcare industry. As Eli Lilly continues to invest in research and development, expand its product offerings, and embrace cutting-edge technologies, the company remains well-positioned to deliver strong returns for investors in the long run.

In conclusion, Eli Lilly and Company's strong business strategy, robust financial performance, and commitment to innovation have attracted the attention of prominent billionaire investors. With a diversified product portfolio, attractive valuation, and long-term growth potential, Eli Lilly is poised to continue delivering strong returns for investors. As the company continues to invest in research and development and embrace cutting-edge technologies, Eli Lilly remains a compelling long-term investment opportunity.

data.title: Europe's Battle for Power: A New Ecosystem for Energy-Hungry Firms
data.description:
The European Union's (EU) commitment to net-zero emissions by 2050 has sparked a battle for power, driving the evolution of a new ecosystem for energy-hungry firms. As the EU strives to reduce its dependence on Russian fossil fuels and accelerate the green transition, the energy landscape is shifting, presenting both challenges and opportunities for businesses.



The EU's energy transition plan, REPowerEU, aligns with the Fit for 55 package and the European Green Deal, aiming to rapidly reduce dependence on Russian fossil fuels and fast-forward the green transition. Key milestones include increasing renewable energy deployment, improving energy efficiency, and strengthening energy security and affordability. This ambitious agenda is driving the development of a new ecosystem for energy-hungry firms, as they adapt to the changing energy landscape.

One of the primary challenges in balancing energy security, affordability, and sustainability is energy security and reliability. The EU's dependence on energy imports, particularly from Russia, has been a significant concern, as seen in the recent energy crisis. Diversifying energy sources and reducing reliance on fossil fuels is crucial for energy security. The EU has been working on diversifying its energy supply, as seen in the increase of LNG imports by more than 60% in 2022 compared to the previous year. Additionally, the REPowerEU plan aims to rapidly reduce dependence on Russian fossil fuels and fast-forward the green transition (European Commission, May 18, 2022).

Another challenge is affordability. The energy transition may lead to increased energy costs, which could disproportionately affect low-income households and businesses. The EU has implemented measures to support energy affordability, such as the Social Climate Fund, which aims to help vulnerable households cope with the costs of the energy transition (European Commission, 2021).

Technological advancements and infrastructure are also crucial for the energy transition. The EU has set a target for energy investments of €396 billion per year from 2021 to 2030 and €520-575 billion per year in the subsequent decades until 2050 (European Commission, 2021). Additionally, the EU has established the European Green Deal Investment Plan, which aims to mobilize at least €1 trillion in sustainable investments over the next decade.

Emerging technologies, like nuclear energy, play a crucial role in helping the European Union achieve its net-zero emissions goal. Nuclear power is considered a low-carbon energy source, as it produces minimal greenhouse gas emissions during electricity generation. According to the European Commission, nuclear energy can contribute to the EU's energy mix, providing stable and low-emission electricity, while also supporting the integration of renewable energy sources (RES) and enhancing energy security.

In conclusion, Europe's battle for power is spurring the evolution of a new ecosystem for energy-hungry firms. As the EU strives to achieve net-zero emissions by 2050, it is driving the development of a more sustainable and secure energy system. By addressing the challenges of energy security, affordability, and technological advancements, the EU can successfully transition to a low-carbon economy while ensuring the competitiveness of its businesses.

data.title: ASIC Charges Olympian's Brother Over Alleged Crypto Scam
data.description:
Australia's finance watchdog, the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), has charged Brendan Gunn, the brother of Olympian breakdancer Rachael "Raygun" Gunn, with allegedly dealing with money suspected to be proceeds of crime. The charges stem from Gunn's operation of a fiat-to-crypto conversion company, Mormarkets Pty Ltd.

ASIC alleges that Gunn dealt with two bank cheques totaling $181,000, which were the proceeds of four investment amounts made by three victims. The investments were made for conversion into cryptocurrencies through Gunn's company. ASIC claims that Gunn dealt with the proceeds of these investments without the knowledge or consent of the investors.

Gunn was charged with one count of dealing with money that could be reasonably suspected to be the proceeds of crime. He appeared in court on March 4 and is expected to return on April 29. If convicted, Gunn faces up to three years in prison, a $37,800 fine, or both.

ASIC Chair Joe Longo stated that the regulator continues to prioritize scam prevention and detection activity, and will take action where required. The charges against Gunn come as Australian regulators are shifting their focus towards the crypto industry, with plans to tighten regulations and crack down on crypto-related crimes and scams.

In recent months, Australian authorities have taken action against several remittance service providers and crypto exchanges for possible compliance issues. In December 2024, the Australian Transaction Reports and Analysis Center (AUSTRAC) announced its intention to focus on the cryptocurrency industry in 2025, with a particular emphasis on crypto ATM providers who may be flouting Anti-Money Laundering laws.

ASIC has also released a consultation paper on proposed guidance for crypto, placing many digital assets under the category of financial products and requiring firms dealing in crypto to be licensed. These regulatory moves aim to strengthen oversight and protect investors in the rapidly evolving crypto landscape.

data.title: Bybit Launches $100K Robot Race: Trade, Compete, Win
data.description:
Cryptocurrency exchange Bybit has launched a Contract Trading Robot Race, offering a $100,000 USDT prize pool to participants. The event, which began on February 28, 2025, and will run until March 31, 2025, challenges traders to use Bybit's powerful futures trading robots to compete for rankings through different strategy combinations and automated settings.

The competition features three futures robot groups: the "Contract Grid Robot," "Contract Plank," and "Contract Martingale." Participants can choose to join one of these teams, with rankings determined by two group leaderboards and one individual leaderboard. Additionally, participants can enter the "Predict to Win Big Prize" section, where lucky voters have the chance to share 5% of the prize pool.

To be eligible for rewards, participants must meet the minimum futures trading volume and account balance requirements. The prize pool expands with increasing trading volume, up to a maximum of $100,000 USDT.

Bybit's Contract Trading Robot Race is designed to encourage users to explore and utilize the exchange's advanced trading tools and features. The event follows the success of previous Bybit initiatives, such as the Great $USDC Race and the Futures Bot Clash, which have attracted thousands of participants and offered substantial prize pools.

The Robot Race also aligns with Bybit's ongoing efforts to promote innovation and engagement in the cryptocurrency trading community. As the market experiences significant growth and innovation, exchanges like Bybit are focusing on providing advanced trading tools and features to cater to the needs of both novice and experienced traders.

In addition to the Robot Race, Bybit has been expanding its global presence through strategic partnerships and collaborations. The exchange has formed alliances with various blockchain projects and financial institutions to boost crypto innovation and adoption. These partnerships have helped Bybit establish a strong foothold in the global cryptocurrency market and attract a diverse user base.

As the cryptocurrency market continues to evolve, exchanges like Bybit play a crucial role in driving innovation and engagement. The Contract Trading Robot Race is just one example of Bybit's commitment to fostering a vibrant and dynamic trading community. With its competitive prize pool and advanced trading tools, the Robot Race is set to attract a large number of participants and further cement Bybit's position as a leading cryptocurrency exchange.
